items.name: Homerow
items.summary: Homerow — keyboard shortcuts for every button in macOS.
items.why_included: Several commenters recommended it as the keyboard-first way to control macOS without a mouse.
items.evidence: People called it "like vimium for your entire Mac" and said they had been using it for years.

items.name: NeverClick
items.summary: NeverClick — Windows-only mouse replacement with intelligent clickable-zone detection.
items.why_included: A commenter said they recently installed it and liked the intelligent mode.
items.evidence: The thread noted it detects possible clickable zones and is Windows-only.

items.name: waynav
items.summary: waynav — a Wayland port of Keynav for keyboard-driven pointer control.
items.why_included: A commenter linked it as the Wayland port for keyboard-only mouse control.
items.evidence: The thread explicitly pointed to "Wayland port: waynav" alongside other alternatives.

items.name: Tough Things First
items.summary: Tough Things First — Ray Zinn’s book on harder, broader entrepreneurial choices.
items.why_included: A commenter recommended it as a worthwhile book on a similar subject.
items.evidence: The thread called it down-to-earth and approachable, not just another startup cliché.

items.name: Remind
items.summary: Remind — a reminder app that inspired a vibe-coded rebuild.
items.why_included: A commenter said their own reminder app was inspired by it.
items.evidence: The thread explicitly named Remind as the original inspiration for their project.

items.name: NASA RELL
items.source_story_title: Astronauts told to return to ISS after sheltering over air leak repairs
items.summary: NASA RELL — a robotic tool for finding and confirming ISS external leaks.
items.why_included: A commenter flagged it as a useful real-world leak detection tool.
items.evidence: They quoted NASA’s fact sheet describing the Robotic External Leak Locator and linked the PDF.

items.name: pg_durable
items.source_story_title: pg_durable: Microsoft open sources in-database durable execution
items.summary: pg_durable — Microsoft’s in-database durable execution layer for Postgres.
items.why_included: Commenters treated it as a real durability primitive with useful OSS underpinnings.
items.evidence: The thread linked Postgreisenough and duroxide, then discussed versioning and lifecycle.

items.name: DBOS
items.source_story_title: pg_durable: Microsoft open sources in-database durable execution
items.summary: DBOS — a Postgres-native workflow and queue platform mentioned as an alternative.
items.why_included: A commenter grouped it with pgQue as part of the emerging Postgres queue stack.
items.evidence: The thread said “2026 is the year of the Postgres queue” and named DBOS directly.

items.name: BliSwitch
items.source_story_title: I tested every IP KVM in my Homelab
items.summary: BliSwitch — BliKVM’s switch accessory for IP KVM setups.
items.why_included: A commenter noticed BliKVM was now making the BLiSwitch hardware.
items.evidence: The thread linked the BliSwitch v2 guide and treated it as a new BliKVM product.

items.name: word-weasel
items.source_story_title: Conventional Commits encourages focus on the wrong things
items.summary: word-weasel — a natural-language linter for technical writing.
items.why_included: A commenter shared it as a tool for cleaner commit messages.
items.evidence: They said they wrote it to teach teams meaningful technical writing.

items.name: Unsloth Studio
items.source_story_title: Gemma 4 QAT models: Optimizing compression for mobile and laptop efficiency
items.summary: Unsloth Studio — a local GUI for running, training, and exporting open models.
items.why_included: A commenter said it worked well for web search and structured JSON on phones.
items.evidence: The thread praised Unsloth’s Gemma quants and said Studio/API were working well in practice.
